rationale_en: A land-use bylaw inside free zones is a narrow administrative rule with no direct investment-incentive lever, so it would not be expected to move province-level investment values.
A free-zone land-use bylaw touches only a small subset of national FDI and sets no fiscal or ownership incentive, so no visible effect on national FDI inflows is expected.
Issuing treasury settlement bonds does add to recorded government debt, but as one settlement mechanism among many financing instruments it is a marginal force against the aggregate debt stock.
Temporary duty-suspended import of raw materials is a narrow customs procedure for a specific input class, not a change to the tariff schedule or import composition at large.
Nursing pay and tariff adjustments affect retention incentives at the margin but do not drive the size of the nursing workforce, which is set by training and hiring capacity.
Nursing incentive pay is a real addition to public health spending but one narrow wage line among the many components of government health expenditure.
This law is the specific mechanism reallocating oil and gas revenue shares to producing provinces, giving it a real if partial channel into provincial capital-expenditure execution shares.
Voiding endowment land deeds affects only a narrow subset of agricultural land and water rights, too small to be expected to move national land or irrigation aggregates.
A single 1960 increase in agricultural credit appropriations is a small, one-off financing measure against the scale of national bank credit and agricultural value added.
Adding one clause to the state-revenue-collection law is a narrow administrative tweak with negligible expected effect on total or other-tax revenue.
This is an explicit customs-duty exemption on vegetable oil itself, a major direct lever on the cost and volume of trade in the exact commodity this chart tracks.
Cheaper imported vegetable oil is a real but indirect substitute-good pressure on the producer price of competing oilcrops, one force among several.
A single tranche of participation bonds under one budget note is a small, one-off financing instrument, negligible against aggregate government debt.

Selling a slice of government-owned housing to occupants is a narrow, historically bounded divestiture too small to move the national tenure-type mix, especially outside the chart's own 2006-2016 window.
This is a procedural amendment to industrial-estate ownership-transfer rules, an administrative tweak with no direct lever on provincial output or workshop counts.
A single-article amendment to enterprise valuation and marketing procedures is a real but narrow procedural piece of the broader Article 44 privatization pipeline feeding stock-market listings.
The quick-return small-enterprise credit program was a real, nationally scaled subsidized-lending and job-creation push, though this entry amends only specific notes within it.

A guaranteed feed-in-tariff purchase mechanism is the direct, named policy lever that creates and sustains independent renewable power generation, so a material effect on renewable output is expected.
The feed-in-tariff mechanism targets renewable generation specifically and has no direct channel to economy-wide per-capita consumption or electrification access.
A nationwide water and wastewater tariff regulation is a real, recurring input into the housing/utilities CPI component, though one of many periodic tariff revisions.

This directive is the core procedural gateway for listing and issuing securities, a real structural channel into listed-company counts and traded value even though many other forces also drive market capitalization.
Establishing the OTC (Fara Bourse) market created an entirely new listing venue, a structural change expected to produce a visible jump in listed-company counts and traded value.
A narrow group credit-insurance scheme under one budget note is a minor risk-reduction tool, too small to be expected to move aggregate private-sector credit.
This bylaw mandates the tariff rate for the fire-insurance class specifically, a direct pricing lever for that class though one of several tariff revisions over time.
Setting the minimum premium and coverage floor for compulsory third-party motor insurance directly prices a major insurance line, though it is one of several such tariff-setting bylaws.
Striking down regulator-set airfares liberalizes pricing and could support demand, but passenger volumes are driven mainly by fleet capacity, sanctions and incomes, not fare regulation alone.
This is the companion ruling in the same 2003 airfare-deregulation episode, with the same partial, indirect expected effect on passenger volumes.
This ruling directly orders that pension payouts be adjusted in line with inflation, a major and direct lever on the real value of the exact 'amounts paid' series this chart tracks.
Annulling one clause of one electricity-market resolution is a narrow legal correction with no expected visible effect on consumption or renewable-output levels.
This is a licensing and registration oversight bylaw for livestock operations, a regulatory backdrop rather than a production-boosting instrument.
Tightening vehicle import technical standards is a classic protectionist lever that can shift competitive advantage toward domestic producers, a real but partial effect among many industry drivers.
These are the operational rules executing the entire FY1390 national budget, the direct instrument determining that year's budget totals and expenditure execution.
As one year's budget-execution rules, this is a real but partial force against the much larger $400bn-scale economy that GDP measures.
Amending duty collection within free zones is a narrow revenue mechanism confined to a small share of total trade-tax collection.
An airport departure fee on outbound travelers is a minor, narrowly scoped revenue instrument with negligible weight in other-tax revenue.
A transport subsidy for exports in special cases only is a small, narrowly targeted trade-promotion tool, too minor to move total merchandise export volumes.

The cabotage rule reserves domestic coastal trade to Iranian-flag vessels, a long-standing legal backdrop rather than an active driver of fleet capacity or transport-services trade in the chart's 2005-2024 window.
This is a downstream 2025 amendment to the subsidy-targeting disbursement mechanism, a real lever on inflation and transfer spending but narrower than the original nationwide price reset.
Cash-transfer levels set under this amendment affect household disposable income, a real but partial input alongside many other drivers of poverty and inequality.
This is the same substantive amendment to the subsidy-targeting disbursement mechanism as its companion filing, with the same real but partial channel to inflation and transfer spending.
Same cash-transfer channel as the companion filing, a real but partial input to household income and poverty measures.
This determination is the direct legal instrument fixing the applied tariff schedule for FY1389, though its effect on import volumes is diluted by many other trade-flow determinants.
This is the same type of direct tariff-rate-fixing instrument for FY1388, with the same strong effect on tariff-rate series and more diluted effect on import volumes.
Amending three procedural articles of the trade-regulations bylaw is a narrow administrative tweak, not a substantive shift in trade policy.
A correction letter to a prior amendment is a procedural clarification with no identifiable substantive channel to trade flows.
Amending the tariff schedule annex directly adjusts specific applied tariff lines, a real but narrow instrument relative to the full tariff-rate average.
This is a general, unspecified amendment to the trade-regulations bylaw with no identified substantive channel to import or export volumes.
Operating regulations for a single free-trade zone are a narrow investment-climate instrument, too small to move national trade-growth rates or business registrations.
This bylaw targets enforcement against smuggling, a symptom of the FX gap, rather than setting exchange rates or trade volumes directly.
A single 400-billion-rial bond tranche is a one-off financing instrument, negligible against the much larger aggregate stock of government debt.
This bylaw governs land leasing for productivity projects, a narrow administrative program with no expected economy-wide effect on the agriculture and rural-development category.

Changing the exchange rate used to value imports for customs purposes mechanically alters the rial value of duties collected on the same physical import volume, a real and fairly direct if periodic effect.
This circular applies an official rate for customs bookkeeping but does not itself set or move the market exchange rate.
Restructuring how confiscation proceeds are split among enforcement agencies is an internal administrative rule with no expected effect on actual trade volumes.
A wartime barter-import arrangement limited to ministries with industrial companies is a narrow, improvised mechanism too small to move aggregate trade flows.
Setting transitional shipping deadlines for already-licensed goods is a narrow customs-timing adjustment with no substantive trade-volume effect.
Clarifying when zone-warehoused goods are deemed to enter customs territory is a technical timing rule, not a substantive trade-policy change.
Revising duty-free allowances for travelers' personal goods is a minor carve-out with no meaningful effect on aggregate import flows.
Mandating a currency-tracking system is an institution-building step toward narrowing the FX gap, but does not itself set or move the exchange rate.
Letting exporters use part of their retained FX earnings for imports is a real incentive at the margin but does not set or move the exchange rate itself.
This directive creates a separate banking and FX regime confined to free zones, an institutional carve-out too small to be expected to move the broader financial sector.
Restricting arbitrary licensing demands is a real ease-of-doing-business measure within the broader Article 44 program, one lever among several shaping the investment climate.

These rules are the direct mechanism through which privatized state companies were floated on the exchange, a structural channel into capital-market development though one of several drivers of listings.
This is a drought-relief and compensation bylaw responding to an already-occurring shock, not a lever that would be expected to reverse or visibly alter agricultural production trends.
This operationalizes the same drought-relief scheme with dedicated levies and credit lines, a real but relief-oriented mechanism unlikely to show up as a visible break in production data.
Reallocating a small sum between line items of the drought-compensation budget table is a trivial administrative correction.
Enforcing a VAT exemption on crude vegetable oil directly lowers the retail cost of this staple, a real but moderate price lever on its consumption.
A VAT exemption confined to one narrow commodity is too small a share of the tax base to be expected to move aggregate goods-and-services tax revenue.
Nationwide environmental compliance standards raise costs and licensing hurdles for miners, a real but marginal constraint unlikely to be visible in aggregate mining production.
This downstream amendment to the subsidy-targeting disbursement bylaw is a real lever on subsidy and transfer spending, though narrower than the original 2010 reform.
As one amendment within the broader subsidy-targeting policy family, this has a real but partial and indirect link to headline inflation.

This law renews the government's statutory authority to set customs duty and profit rates each year, a structurally important but enabling (not rate-fixing) instrument for the tariff and customs-duty series.
Raising a budget-year FX ceiling and permitting use of oil reserve funds does not itself set or move the market exchange rate.
This provision explicitly authorizes using a quantified $2.5bn sum to prevent an increase in the government's central-bank debt, a direct if one-time channel to this exact series.

This is an economy-wide, explicitly anti-inflation price-freeze law voiding administered price increases nationwide, a major instrument expected to produce a visible if temporary effect on the price indices.
The same law mandates a quantified diversion of agency budgets into the subsidy line, a direct if bounded addition to subsidy and transfer spending.
A duty-drawback refund limited to raw materials used by domestic machine manufacturers is a narrow instrument, too small to move aggregate customs-duty revenue.
Exempting exported cement from customs duties is a real but narrow export-promotion channel; domestic cement production is driven mainly by domestic demand and capacity, not export incentives.
This is a commercial-profit-rate determination bylaw of the same kind that directly fixes applied tariff rates, though undated and less clearly tied to a single fiscal year.

Corporatizing provincial electricity distribution is a real governance reform that can improve operational efficiency and open the door to private participation, but is only one of several forces shaping those outcomes.

This is the primary drought-compensation law responding to a severe multi-year drought; it can soften the shock to farmers but the drought itself, not the relief law, drives the shape of wheat and cereal output.

This law directly governs how the remaining balance of oil signing-bonus funds is allocated among recipients within the exact 1955-63 window these charts track, a major instrument for this specific allocation series.
As one year's development-financing law during the Third Plan era, this is a real but partial force in an economy where many other public and private investment flows also mattered.
Consolidating fertilizer distribution under the agriculture ministry reorganizes a key input-supply channel, a real but indirect and partial effect on fertilizer use levels.
A recurring quarterly quota announcement for a niche category of passenger-accompanying goods in free zones is a routine administrative notice, not a trade-shaping instrument.
This interpretive act resolves a legal ambiguity for a narrow subset of pre-1993 exporter FX commitments, with no substantive channel to the exchange rate or export volumes.
This ruling blocks one narrow clause raising gas prices only for above-average consumption, too small a price action to be expected to move headline inflation or national gas demand data.
This chart's totals are compiled directly from the enacted annual budget law, so the FY1385 budget act is the primary source document defining the series itself.
As one year's routine national budget, this is a real but partial force among the many determinants of that year's GDP, growth and inflation figures.
This chart's totals are compiled directly from the enacted annual budget law, so the FY1376 budget act is the primary source document defining the series itself.
As one year's routine national budget, this is a real but partial force among the many determinants of that year's GDP, growth and inflation figures.
This is a technical correction to the credit tables annexed to the Fourth Development Plan, a procedural amendment rather than a substantive change to the investment programme itself.
As a primary annual budget act, this is a real but partial force against the broader determinants of that year's GDP, expenditure and price level.

This amends the structural statute governing how government agencies collect and dispose of revenue and manage treasury procedures, a real but partial effect spread across many broad fiscal aggregates.
This adjusts specific spending tables of the FY1384 supplementary budget, a real but incremental revision to that year's totals rather than the primary budget act.
As the primary annual budget act for a formative year of the modern Iranian fiscal state, this is real historical context but, like any one year's budget, only a partial force against GDP.
This is the same structural amendment to the State Financial Regulations framework as its companion filing, with the same real but partial effect across broad fiscal aggregates.
This amends specific allocations of the FY1382 budget, a real but incremental revision to that year's totals rather than the primary budget act.

This law establishes the legal and fee regime for transit trade, a real and named channel into transit logistics and transport-services exports, though logistics performance is shaped by many other factors too.
A bilateral trade agreement with a small partner economy (Bahrain) is too narrow to be expected to move Iran's aggregate goods-and-services trade growth.
